evidence: Boundary split in ProjectContext is partially bypassed by a compatibility facade: `useProject()` recombines selection+crud+identity into one wide contract (`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx:135-145`) and is still used broadly across feature and domain code (e.g. `src/domains/generation/hooks/useDeleteGenerationAction.ts:15`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:138`).

UI layering contract is inconsistent: `UI_Z_LAYERS` centralizes z-order (`src/shared/lib/uiLayers.ts:2-7`), but lightbox interaction guards still hardcode `100000` checks (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts:18,26`) instead of using that contract.

Open-state contracts are duplicated instead of shared: `OpenStateContract` exists (`src/shared/components/dialogs/contracts.ts:2-5`) while domain-specific `DeleteGenerationConfirmContract` redefines the same shape (`src/domains/generation/contracts/deleteGenerationConfirm.ts:1-4`) and is threaded through hooks/UI (`src/domains/generation/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.ts:6-12`, `src/shared/components/dialogs/DeleteGenerationConfirmDialog.tsx:2-15`).

Tooltip policy is scattered: global provider uses delay 300 (`src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx:54`), while nested providers reintroduce defaults or divergent delays (`src/app/App.tsx:59`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx:520`, multiple feature files via search).

Mechanical signal check: `god modules` and `boundary violations` are confirmed (ProjectContext compatibility surface + broad consumers). `import-time side effects` are mostly refuted for the seed runtime path: initialization is explicitly gated in bootstrap (`src/app/bootstrap.tsx:52-110`). `deferred import pressure` from `src/domains/generation/types/index.ts` is largely acceptable because exports are type-only (`src/domains/generation/types/index.ts:5-13`).

Travel task payload reading is split across multiple modules with different precedence orders: `taskParamsUtils.ts` defines `TRAVEL_*_SOURCE_ORDER` arrays while `travelContractData.ts` defines separate `CONTRACT_SOURCES`/`LEGACY_SOURCES` and a different fallback sequence.

`useAdjustedTaskDetails.ts` reimplements a local `deriveInputImages` helper instead of using shared task-param readers, creating a narrower extraction path than `taskParamsUtils.ts` and `travelContractData.ts`.

Lightbox shared-state abstractions require large param bags and pass-through mapping (`ImageLightbox.tsx` -> `buildImageSharedLightboxInput` -> `useSharedLightboxState.ts`, mirrored by `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`), increasing indirection and local reasoning cost.

Mechanical concern confirmation: abstraction hotspots are present and material. Mechanical concern refutation: no concrete dependency cycle was confirmed in reviewed paths; imports are one-directional in sampled hotspots (`taskParamsUtils` -> `tasks/*`, lightbox render hooks -> shared hooks/components) with no reciprocal imports found.

`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeHandler.ts` is the shared bootstrap/wrapper used by many edge functions (`get-task-output`, `complete_task`, `claim-next-task`, `setup-auto-topup`, etc.), but there are no tests referencing `bootstrapEdgeHandler` or `withEdgeRequest`.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-core.test.ts` only checks exported symbol existence; it does not validate query behavior, error handling, or idempotency paths in `findExistingGeneration`, `findSourceGenerationByImageUrl`, `createVariant`, and `linkGenerationToShot`.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/taskParamContract.ts` is consumed by server and client flows (`supabase/functions/complete_task/params.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`) but has no dedicated tests for path precedence, coercion, and null/undefined semantics.

Mechanical scan signal for `src/shared/lib/tasks/taskParamContract.ts` (test_coverage/export pressure) is confirmed by absence of matching tests; signal for `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` is partially refuted because it has direct unit tests, though project-scope edge branches remain lightly covered.

API contract drift risk is visible in `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation-core.ts`: `contains('tasks', JSON.stringify([taskId]))` is untested and no integration test verifies that this filter actually matches persisted `tasks` payload shapes.
Batch-level `concern_signals` for AI Debt & Migrations were empty in the packet, but scan-evidence leads in the same subsystem confirmed migration debt patterns.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines policy-backed enforcement helpers (`collectTravelStructureLegacyUsage`, `enforceTravelStructureLegacyPolicy`) that are not used by production callers; `rg` finds only tests using them.

Legacy field handling is duplicated and diverging across files: strict rejection in `payloadBuilder.ts` (`assertNoImplicitLegacyStructureFields`) while read/adaptation code in ShotEditor services continues permissive multi-shape parsing.

findings.summary: Split project contexts are undermined by a still-dominant aggregate `useProject()` facade, preserving high coupling and broad ripple risk.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate `useProject()` behind a migration plan, convert consumers to narrow hooks (`useProjectSelectionContext`, `useProjectCrudContext`, `useProjectIdentityContext`), and enforce via lint rule to prevent new aggregate-context usage.
evidence: `useProject()` merges all three contexts back into one object (`ProjectContext.tsx:135-145`), nullifying intended ownership decomposition.
Domain hook consumes the aggregate context (`useDeleteGenerationAction.ts:15`) despite only needing `selectedProjectId`.
Large UI surfaces also consume aggregate context (`MediaGalleryItem.tsx:138`), increasing cross-cutting dependency spread.

findings.summary: Lightbox interaction logic bypasses the shared z-layer contract with hard-coded numeric thresholds.
findings.suggestion: Replace numeric checks with a shared helper derived from `UI_Z_LAYERS` (e.g., `isAboveLayer(UI_Z_LAYERS.LIGHTBOX_MODAL)`), and route all lightbox overlay precedence checks through that helper.
evidence: Interaction guards compare against literal `100000` (`useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts:18,26`).
The codebase already defines canonical layer constants (`uiLayers.ts:2-7`) and the shell uses them (`LightboxShell.tsx:125,156`).
Changing layer values in one place can silently desync close-prevention behavior in another.

findings.summary: Dialog open/close contract is duplicated across domain and shared layers, creating avoidable interface drift.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on `OpenStateContract` composition for all dialog-like contracts (`extends OpenStateContract`) and move confirm-specific extensions to shared dialog contract helpers to reduce repeated interface definitions.
evidence: `OpenStateContract` already defines `open` + `onOpenChange` (`dialogs/contracts.ts:2-5`).
`DeleteGenerationConfirmContract` redeclares the same fields and adds `onConfirm` (`deleteGenerationConfirm.ts:1-4`).
Hook composes and exports this domain-local duplicate contract (`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.ts:6-12`), reinforcing parallel contract paths.

findings.summary: Tooltip behavior policy is not centrally enforced, with nested providers and inconsistent delay values across the app.
findings.suggestion: Define one app-level tooltip policy provider and ban feature-level providers unless explicitly justified; expose a small override API for exceptional cases instead of ad-hoc nested providers.
evidence: Global provider sets `delayDuration={300}` (`AppProviders.tsx:54`).
App content wraps an additional provider with default delay (`App.tsx:59` -> `tooltip.tsx` default 120ms).
Feature components add further nested providers (`MediaGallery/index.tsx:520` and many additional occurrences found by search), making tooltip behavior dependent on tree position.

findings.summary: Travel payload extraction uses conflicting precedence contracts across modules.
findings.suggestion: Create one canonical travel payload selector contract (single module exporting field-level precedence tables and typed selectors), then migrate `taskParamsUtils`, `travelContractData`, and segment/task-detail consumers to that shared selector API.
evidence: `taskParamsUtils.ts` defines custom source orders (`TRAVEL_SEGMENT_IMAGE_SOURCE_ORDER`, `TRAVEL_TIMELINE_IMAGE_SOURCE_ORDER`, `TRAVEL_PROMPT_SOURCE_ORDER`) and calls `createTravelPayloadReader` directly with those arrays.

`travelContractData.ts` independently defines `CONTRACT_SOURCES` and `LEGACY_SOURCES`, with a different legacy order (`orchestratorDetails`, `rawParams`, `fullOrchestratorPayload`) and separate fallback logic.

`individualTravelSegment.ts` consumes `readTravelContractData(snapshot)` while other UI paths call `deriveInputImages`/`derivePrompt`, so equivalent fields can resolve differently depending on caller.

findings.summary: Variant task details use a local input-image parser that diverges from shared logic.
findings.suggestion: Delete the local helper in `useAdjustedTaskDetails.ts` and replace it with shared task-payload readers (`parseTaskParams` + `deriveInputImages` or a canonical travel selector) so variant and task-details views resolve images identically.
evidence: `useAdjustedTaskDetails.ts` defines a private `deriveInputImages` helper that only checks `input_images`, `orchestrator_details.input_images`, and start/end image URLs.

Shared parser `taskParamsUtils.ts` handles additional travel fields (`input_image_paths_resolved`, `image_urls`, `individual_segment_params`, contract-aware precedence), so the local helper can omit valid sources.

`VideoTravelDetails.tsx` already uses shared readers (`deriveInputImages`, `buildTaskPayloadSnapshot`, `readTravelContractData`), meaning the lightbox variant path and task-details path are not contract-aligned for the same task payload.

findings.summary: Lightbox state assembly relies on oversized param bags and delegation-heavy facades.
findings.suggestion: Replace the monolithic lightbox facade input/output with narrower domain hooks (variants, shot actions, layout, task panel) consumed directly by image/video render models; keep shared primitives but remove pass-through object assembly layers.
evidence: `useSharedLightboxState.ts` defines multi-bag input (`core`, `navigation`, `shots`, `layout`, `actions`, `media`) and a large nested return surface, then composes additional sub-facades (`useSharedVariantsState`, navigation/shot/presentation models).
`ImageLightbox.tsx` builds `sharedInput` via `buildImageSharedLightboxInput` by forwarding many fields with minimal transformation, creating an explicit wrapper layer before real behavior.

`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` repeats similar large-object assembly (`lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, workflow/panel props), indicating recurring indirection instead of direct, narrow contracts per concern.

findings.summary: Shared edge bootstrap wrapper has no direct tests despite being a cross-function entry contract.
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeHandler.test.ts` table-driven suite covering: OPTIONS preflight, method mismatch, strict/loose parse failures, auth required/optional, service-role rejection, handler-throw fallback, and logger flush in both success and failure paths.
evidence: `withEdgeRequest` and `bootstrapEdgeHandler` implement method gating, parse modes, auth/service-role checks, and flush behavior, but `rg` over `supabase/functions/**/*.test.ts` shows no tests targeting these shared functions directly.
At least 10+ handlers call this shared path, so a regression in one branch (e.g., strict/loose parse or `errorResponseFormat`) would fan out across unrelated endpoints.

findings.summary: Generation core persistence/query behavior is unvalidated beyond export smoke tests.
findings.suggestion: Replace export-only tests with behavior tests using mocked Supabase query builders: verify `contains` argument shape, `PGRST116` handling, non-`PGRST116` fallback behavior, and that de-duplication callers receive expected results for existing/non-existing tasks.
evidence: `generation-core.test.ts` only asserts functions are defined; it does not execute any DB interaction logic.

`findExistingGeneration` uses `.contains('tasks', JSON.stringify([taskId]))` and catches errors to return `null`, so incorrect filter semantics or query errors can silently disable de-duplication paths used by generation handlers.

findings.summary: Cross-runtime task param contract paths/coercions are centralized but not directly tested.
findings.suggestion: Add direct unit tests for `taskParamContract.ts` with matrix cases for each path set and coercion branch, then keep wrapper tests in `params.test.ts` minimal to prevent duplicated-but-incomplete coverage.
evidence: `TASK_PARAM_CONTRACT_PATHS` encodes multi-shape precedence for orchestrator IDs, run IDs, shot IDs, and booleans, but there is no `taskParamContract` test file.

Current `complete_task/params.test.ts` validates a few extraction examples through wrappers, but does not cover precedence conflicts, boolean string/number coercion matrix (`'0'`, `'1'`, `0`, `1`, invalid strings), or null/undefined fallthrough behavior.

findings.summary: Text-mode edge responses are used in production handlers but lack contract tests for envelope consistency.
findings.suggestion: Add endpoint-level contract tests (or shared wrapper tests) that assert text-mode response status/body for 400/401/403/405/500 paths, and explicitly lock expected divergence from JSON envelope behavior.
evidence: `edgeRequest.test.ts` validates JSON parse/error helpers, but there is no test that `bootstrapEdgeHandler` in `errorResponseFormat: 'text'` mode preserves expected status/messages across method/auth/parse failures.
`get-orchestrator-children` and `claim-next-task` both opt into text-mode errors, creating a separate API surface from JSON-edge functions without shared contract tests.

findings.summary: Legacy-structure deprecation policy is declared but not enforced in runtime paths, leaving migration governance as dead scaffolding.
findings.suggestion: Choose one migration boundary and wire enforcement there (read adapters, task creation, or both). Either invoke `collect/enforce` in real paths and emit actionable telemetry, or delete the unused policy helpers and keep a single explicit validation path to reduce fake-governance debt.
evidence: `legacyStructureVideo.ts` exports `collectTravelStructureLegacyUsage` and `enforceTravelStructureLegacyPolicy` with deprecation owner/remove-by metadata.
Repository search shows those functions are referenced only in `legacyStructureVideo.test.ts`, not in production call sites.
Actual runtime behavior is split: canonical builder rejects legacy fields (`assertNoImplicitLegacyStructureFields`), while apply-settings read path still parses multiple legacy aliases.

findings.summary: Structure-video migration code overwrites loaded structure type to `uni3c`, causing lossy adaptation across legacy and mixed data.
findings.suggestion: Preserve existing `structure_type` during migration unless an explicit versioned migration rule transforms it. If uni3c-only is intended, tighten shared types and remove non-uni3c branches in extraction/adapters to make contracts honest.
evidence: In `migrateToArrayFormat`, when `settings.structure_videos` exists, each entry is remapped with `structure_type: 'uni3c'` regardless of input value.
The declared contract still allows `'uni3c' | 'flow' | 'canny' | 'depth'` for structure type in shared travel-between-images types.
Apply-settings extraction also reads and infers non-uni3c structure types, so this forced rewrite creates internal inconsistency and potential data loss.

findings.summary: Motion-mode unions are narrowed in editor apply-settings flow, creating contract drift against shared task payload types.
findings.suggestion: Unify motion-mode contract across shared types and UI adapter types. Either normalize `'presets' -> 'basic'` at a single boundary with explicit mapping utility/tests, or restore full union support end-to-end.
evidence: Shared travel task contracts type `motion_mode` as `'basic' | 'presets' | 'advanced'`.
Apply-settings `ExtractedSettings.motionMode`, callback signatures, and casts are narrowed to `'basic' | 'advanced'`.
Settings comments indicate presets were merged into basic, but shared contracts still advertise `presets`, leaving migration/readback semantics ambiguous.

findings.summary: Image preloader full-mode URL selection ignores `location`, skipping valid media entries that do not populate `url`.
findings.suggestion: Use shared URL resolution logic (`location`/`url`/`imageUrl` precedence) in `getImageUrl` and add tests for `location`-only and mixed-shape media objects.
evidence: `PreloadableImage` includes `location`, but `getImageUrl` in `preloader.ts` uses only `img.url` when `thumbnailOnly` is false.
Elsewhere in the codebase media URL resolution commonly falls back to `location` (e.g., helper patterns and travel UI components).
Current preloader tests cover `url`, `thumbUrl`, and `thumbnail_url`, but do not include a `location`-only case, allowing this mismatch to persist.

findings.summary: Generation domain ownership is split between `domains/generation` and `shared/lib`, obscuring package boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Move generation scope/mapping services into `src/domains/generation/repository` (or a new `src/domains/generation/services`) and expose a small domain public surface; keep `shared` only for truly domain-agnostic primitives.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations` in `src/domains/generation/hooks` imports `resolveGenerationProjectScope` and `resolveVariantProjectScope` from `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`.
`generationTaskRepository.ts` contains generation/variant scope resolution and mapping logic that is generation-domain specific, not cross-domain generic utility.
`useLineageChain` also imports `resolveVariantProjectScope` from the same shared file, increasing cross-feature dependence on a mislocated domain service.

findings.summary: A UI hook bridge for generation-task mapping is placed in `shared/lib`, mixing package roles.
findings.suggestion: Relocate this hook bridge into a domain or feature hook package (for example `src/domains/generation/hooks`) and keep repository accessors in repository modules; reserve `shared/lib` for non-React, cross-domain utilities.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge.ts` exports `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` (React Query mutation hook) from `shared/lib`, which is typically a non-React utility layer.

The bridge only wraps `getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` from `generationTaskRepository` and is used by `useGenerationTaskDetails`, indicating UI-adapter behavior rather than foundational shared library behavior.
This creates a layered mismatch (`shared/lib` contains React hook orchestration while underlying data access also lives there).

findings.summary: Home-hero-only visual component is stored under global shared components.
findings.suggestion: Move `ConstellationCanvas` into `src/pages/Home/components/hero` (or a local `visuals` subfolder) and keep a shared version only if a second real consumer appears.
evidence: `HomeBackground.tsx` imports `ConstellationCanvas` from shared, and repo search shows no other production importer.
`ConstellationCanvas` implements page-atmosphere animation tightly coupled to home hero presentation rather than reusable app UI.

findings.summary: Project creation flow logic is packaged as a shared component despite app-specific navigation/context coupling.
findings.suggestion: Move project-creation modal + controller behavior into an app-shell/global-header feature package (for example `src/features/layout` or `src/shared/components/GlobalHeader` subtree) and leave `shared/components` for reusable, flow-agnostic pieces.
evidence: `CreateProjectModal` uses `useProject()` context and hardcoded `navigate('/tools')`, making it app-flow specific.
Primary usage is from `GlobalHeader`, suggesting ownership belongs with header/app-shell feature rather than generic shared components.
Keeping this in shared blurs whether shared components are reusable primitives or feature workflow units.

findings.summary: Prompt-editor-specific controls are top-level shared components instead of colocated with their owning feature.
findings.suggestion: Colocate `BulkEditControls` under a prompt-editor module folder (for example `src/shared/components/PromptEditorModal/components` or a dedicated feature package) and expose only a minimal entry from that owner module.
evidence: `BulkEditControls` is imported by `PromptEditorModal` and is tightly coupled to prompt-editing AI semantics (`AIModelType`, bulk edit instruction workflow).
Current placement as a top-level shared component weakens discoverability of prompt editor ownership and increases shared directory sprawl.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase migration gate checks a stale allowlist count, not real import usage.
findings.suggestion: Replace allowlist-count enforcement with a real source scan (same pattern used by `runtimeErrorAliasGovernance.contract.test.ts`), then keep allowlist optional as migration metadata only. Fail CI when scanned importer count exceeds budget or when allowlist contains non-existent paths.
evidence: Governance test computes `importerCount` from `legacySupabaseAllowlist.json` and compares to budget; it does not scan source imports.
Current production import count for `@/integrations/supabase/client` is 151, while allowlist has 128 entries.
Allowlist includes malformed entry (`src/shared/hoo../../compat/hooks/useExternalApiKeys.ts`), proving drift can exist without failing governance checks.

findings.summary: Generation type barrel is documented as canonical but deep type imports bypass it.
findings.suggestion: Add an import-governance contract test (or lint rule) that forbids deep imports under `@/domains/generation/types/*` outside the types package, then migrate current deep-import callers to `@/domains/generation/types`.
evidence: `index.ts` states imports should point to this public surface for compatibility.
Production modules import internals directly (`@/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow`, `.../generationParams`, `.../shotViewRow`) instead of barrel.
No contract test was found that enforces barrel-only imports for generation types.

findings.summary: Many tests are shallow and mock deprecated Supabase shape, weakening change-detection quality.
findings.suggestion: Replace placeholder assertions with behavior-level checks and standardize a canonical Supabase client test helper that mocks `getSupabaseClient`/`getSupabaseClientResult` only; add a lint/check to reject `vi.mock('@/integrations/supabase/client', () => ({ supabase: ... }))` patterns.
evidence: Representative tests assert only symbol presence and tautologies (`expect(true).not.toBe(false)`), giving near-zero behavioral coverage.
Several tests mock `@/integrations/supabase/client` with `{ supabase: ... }` even though contract test expects no `supabase` export from canonical client facade.
This mismatch means tests can pass while real runtime integration contracts drift, because mocks preserve old APIs independent of production module shape.

findings.summary: Large UI hubs mix domain transforms, orchestration, and rendering in single components
findings.suggestion: Split each hub into explicit boundaries: extract data/view-model hooks (filter/sort/pagination or lightbox state derivation), isolate action controllers, and keep top-level components as composition shells with narrow props.
evidence: PhilosophyPane embeds inline animation CSS, complex layout branching for multiple demo modes, media wiring, and CTA/navigation concerns in one component.
BrowsePresetsTab performs resource merge/dedup/filter/sort/pagination and full card UI/action rendering in a single file-level unit.
MediaGalleryLightbox centralizes many independent contracts and adaptation layers rather than delegating to cohesive feature subcomponents.

findings.summary: Lightbox internals rely on manual mirrored state and cache subscription choreography
findings.suggestion: Introduce a dedicated `useLightboxShotSelection`/`useLightboxStarState` controller that owns synchronization semantics and exposes a stable API to both lightbox and shot-action UI, removing manual cache-version forcing and duplicated shot-selection transitions.
evidence: MediaGalleryLightbox keeps `lightboxSelectedShotId` local state, resets it from parent in effect and close-path callbacks, then rethreads it through adapter callbacks.
The same module subscribes directly to React Query cache events and increments a version counter to force recomputation (`void cacheVersion`).
ShotActions depends on parallel local selector/open state contracts, showing boundary friction between gallery and lightbox state ownership.

findings.summary: No-op diagnostic plumbing adds ceremony without runtime value
findings.suggestion: Replace placeholder/no-op constructs with concrete behavior: either wire `reportMapperIssue` to a real dev logger or remove it and simplify callsites; replace `void cacheVersion` with an explicit derived selector or subscription hook returning the needed data.
evidence: generationParamsMapper keeps `reportMapperIssue` with discarded parameters (`void context; void logData;`), suggesting placeholder instrumentation left in production paths.
MediaGalleryLightbox uses a synthetic dependency touch (`void cacheVersion`) instead of explicit dataflow, increasing cognitive noise.

findings.summary: Behavior-heavy components are guarded mainly by import/existence tests
findings.suggestion: Replace surface tests with behavior-focused suites: render interactions and assertions for core branches (preset filtering/sorting/paging, lightbox shot-change sync, pane button matrix by mode, stroke draw/drag/resize transitions). Prioritize deterministic integration tests at component boundaries.
evidence: Each listed test primarily checks `toBeDefined()`/module import and does not validate user-visible behavior or branch contracts.
These correspond to large/high-branch files where regressions are likely in filtering/sorting/paging, pointer gestures, state sync, and optimistic updates.
